stem: A school's local area network uses several pieces of hardware.
review_notes: Shuffle-test fixture. Larger (5-row) matching widget so the longer SVG overlay exposes any residual drift in line alignment. The right column is deliberately longer than the left so display-order shuffling is visible.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Match each network device to the job it performs on a LAN.

right: Forwards packets of data between different networks.
Forwards frames only to the port of the intended device on the LAN.
Lets a single computer connect physically to a network.
Provides Wi-Fi connectivity to devices within its range.
Provides shared resources such as files or printers to clients.
Amplifies a weak signal so it can travel further down a cable.
Connects every device in a small network onto one shared segment.

mark_points.text: Switch — forwards frames to the intended port on the LAN
NIC — connects a computer to the network
Server — provides shared resources to clients

misconceptions.label: Switch vs hub
misconceptions.description: A switch sends traffic only to the intended port; the last two right-column options describe a repeater and a hub respectively.
